title: Thai Crispy Chicken Fritters
description: Crispy on the outside, tender on the inside, these Thai-inspired chicken fritters are a delicious snack or appetizer.

ingredients.name: delicate herbs (such as lemongrass, galangal, Thai basil)

instructions: 1. In a large bowl, combine chicken breast, flour, cornstarch, delicate herbs, coconut milk, and crispy rice. Mix well.
2. Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
3. Using a spoon, drop small amounts of the chicken mixture into the hot oil, flattening slightly with a spatula.
4. Fry the fritters for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until golden brown and crispy.
5. Using a slotted spoon, remove the fritters from the oil and place on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
6. In a small bowl, mix together sesame oil and fish sauce. Brush the mixture onto the fritters.
7. Serve the fritters hot with a side of Greek yogurt for dipping.
8. Garnish with additional delicate herbs, if desired.
10. Refrigerate leftover fritters for up to 3 days and reheat in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 5-7 minutes.
11. Freeze fritters for up to 2 months and reheat in the oven at 350°F (175°C) for 10-12 minutes.
12. Note: If you want to make the fritters extra crispy, you can chill them in the refrigerator for 30 minutes before frying.
